stage1_answer:
- Queried Dependency Explorer to enumerate every downstream service that ts-seat-service calls at 2025-07-20 21:41:00 → only ts-config-service and ts-order-other-service.  
- Pulled 20-minute response-time & error-rate metrics for both downstream services: both woke up from zero traffic but stayed < 6 ms and 0 % errors, so they are **not** the source of delay.  
- Pulled the same metrics for ts-seat-service itself: call volume jumped to 2 613, average response-time rose to ~19 ms and error-rate to ~3 %, proving the slowdown is **internal** to the alerting service.  
- No evidence of downstream fault; the latency spike originates inside ts-seat-service when it suddenly receives traffic after a quiet hour.

Root Cause Endpoint: ts-seat-service  
Root Cause Reason: Internal performance bottleneck (CPU, GC, thread-pool or similar) triggered by abrupt traffic surge from zero to 2 613 calls inside 20 min.
final_answer:
Root Cause Endpoint: ts-seat-service  
Root Cause Reason: Internal performance bottleneck (CPU, GC, thread-pool or similar) triggered by abrupt traffic surge from zero to 2 613 calls inside 20 min.
